title: Freeze on Death with Loss Reason Display
content:
## Overview When any snake dies, the game enters a **death freeze** instead of immediately jumping to the Game Over screen. The board stays visible and frozen, the fatal collision cell is highlighted in amber at its correct isometric depth, a pulsing banner names the loss reason, and the game waits for any key press before advancing to the normal Game Over screen. This applies to all modes: human single-player, bot-driven single-player, and bot-vs-bot. Before this feature, `endGame()` / `endGameTwoPlayer()` were called immediately on death, which called `showScreen('game-over')` and set `display:none` on `#game`, hiding the canvas entirely. The fatal head cell was never drawn because collision was checked before `this.snake.unshift(head)` and `draw()` was never called on the death path. --- ## Architecture ### New instance fields (`src/main.ts`) Four fields added alongside the existing `deathCause` field (~line 154): | Field | Type | Purpose | |---|---|---| | `deathReason` | `'wall' \| 'self' \| 'opponent' \| 'head-to-head' \| 'starved'` | Drives the banner text | | `deathCells` | `Array<{ x: number; y: number; who: 1 \| 2 }>` | Fatal head cell(s) to highlight; empty for starvation | | `awaitingDeathAck` | `boolean` | True during the freeze | | `pendingFinalize` | `(() => void) \| null` | Deferred end-game callback | The existing `deathCause: 'collision' | 'starved'` field is **unchanged** — it still drives the Game Over title and winner nuance in `endGame`/`endGameTwoPlayer`. All four new fields are reset in `startNewGame` next to `this.deathCause = 'collision'` (~line 1104), and `hideDeathBanner()` is called beside the pause-hide (~line 1110) so a restart never leaves a stuck banner or stale alarm cell. ### `beginDeathFreeze(finalize: () => void)` (near end-game functions, before line 1801) 1. Sets `isGameOver = true`, `awaitingDeathAck = true`, `pendingFinalize = finalize`. 2. Calls `stopAutoRotate()` and clears the game loop (`clearInterval` + `this.gameLoop = null`) — same lines `endGame` runs, idempotent when `endGame` re-runs them at finalize time. 3. Hides `#starvation-warning` so it doesn't overlap the banner. 4. Calls `this.draw()` to render the frozen board with death cell highlight(s). 5. Calls `this.showDeathBanner()`. ### Four death sites rewired (`src/main.ts`) | Site | Location | `deathReason` | `deathCells` | Callback | |---|---|---|---|---| | Single-player collision | ~line 1161 | `inBounds(head) ? 'self' : 'wall'` | `[{x:head.x, y:head.y, who:1}]` | `() => this.endGame()` | | Single-player starvation | ~line 1183 | `'starved'` | `[]` | `() => this.endGame()` | | Two-player collision | ~line 1231 | derived from collision booleans | `head1` if p1Dead, `head2` if p2Dead | `() => this.endGameTwoPlayer(p1Dead, p2Dead)` | | Two-player starvation | ~line 1275 | `'starved'` | `[]` | `() => this.endGameTwoPlayer(p1Starved, p2Starved)` | For two-player collision, `deathReason` is derived from the already-computed booleans: `headToHead ? 'head-to-head' : (p1HitsWall||p2HitsWall) ? 'wall' : (p1HitsSelf||p2HitsSelf) ? 'self' : 'opponent'`. Per-snake reasons `r1`/`r2` are also computed for the banner text. ### Death cell rendering in `draw()` — painter's algorithm integration The amber death-cell marker is pushed into the **painter's-algorithm object list** as a `'death'` type entry before the list is sorted, so it is drawn at its correct isometric depth relative to surrounding segments. Array sort stability ensures the marker paints over any segment sharing its cell. This prevents the marker from visually floating on top of snake segments that are actually in front of it in the scene. **Bounds handling** branches on the grid rectangle (`x < 0 || x >= gridSize || y < 0 || y >= gridSize`): - **Off the grid rectangle (wall death):** the true cell projects mostly off-canvas. Fallback: draw the alarm block at the snake's current head (`snake[0]`/`snake2[0]`, the last valid cell) via the painter's sort, **and** also draw a sliver at the true OOB coordinate afterward as a special case (outside the sorted list) so it pokes past the border edge as an "off the edge" cue. The off-grid sliver is the only death rendering that still draws on top. - **Within the grid rectangle (self / opponent / head-to-head, or Iron Snake interior void):** projects on-canvas; inserted into the sorted object list directly. This correctly shows Iron Snake deaths where the head entered the interior void. Starvation (`deathCells = []`) is a no-op here — the freeze shows the last live frame plus the banner only. The `drawBlockShadow` calls and duplicated `drawBlock` calls from the old on-top rendering were removed when the marker was moved into the painter's sort. Death cells are re-read on every `draw()` call, so the highlight persists across window resize and any redraws that occur while frozen. ### Banner DOM and CSS - **`index.html`:** `
` added inside `.canvas-wrap` as a sibling after `#starvation-warning` (~line 97). Overlays the canvas without shifting layout; no `showScreen` needed. - **`src/style.css`:** `.death-banner` modeled on `.starvation-warning` (lines 184–206) but centered (`top:50%; transform:translate(-50%,-50%)`), larger, alarm-red, `pointer-events:none`, with its own pulse keyframe. **Banner copy:** | `deathReason` | Text | |---|---| | `wall` | "Hit the wall!" | | `self` | "Ran into itself!" | | `opponent` | "Crashed into the other snake!" | | `head-to-head` | "Head-on collision!" | | `starved` | "Starved!" | Two-player mode composes with P1/P2 labels using per-snake `r1`/`r2` reasons. All messages append " — Press any key". Two methods near `updateStarvationWarning` (~line 1750): `showDeathBanner()` builds and displays the text; `hideDeathBanner()` re-hides it. ### Acknowledgement branch in `handleKeyPress` (`src/main.ts`) Inserted at the very top of `handleKeyPress`, after the INPUT/TEXTAREA/SELECT guard (~line 752) and **before** the `Enter` branch (~line 754): ```typescript if (this.awaitingDeathAck) { if (e.repeat || e.key === 'Shift' || e.key === 'Control' || e.key === 'Alt' || e.key === 'Meta') return e.preventDefault() this.awaitingDeathAck = false this.hideDeathBanner() const finalize = this.pendingFinalize this.pendingFinalize = null finalize?.() return } ``` Ordering is critical: this fires before `Enter`/`r`/`Escape` branches, so during the freeze the first key dismisses to Game Over rather than triggering restart or menu. After `finalize()` runs, `showScreen('game-over')` is called and normal Game Over key handling resumes unchanged. `e.repeat` and bare modifiers are ignored to prevent a stray held key from skipping the freeze instantly. **Any key dismisses the freeze** — including camera/view keys. This is the simplest behavior and matches "until a key is pressed." --- ## Files Modified | File | Changes | |---|---| | `src/main.ts` | New fields; `beginDeathFreeze`, `showDeathBanner`, `hideDeathBanner`; four death sites rewired; death-cell inserted into painter's sort in `draw()`; ack branch in `handleKeyPress`; resets in `startNewGame` | | `index.html` | `#death-banner` div inside `.canvas-wrap` | | `src/style.css` | `.death-banner` styles and pulse keyframe | --- ## Behavioral Notes - `endGame`/`endGameTwoPlayer` run at ack-time (key press), not at the death instant. `saveHighScore`, `maybePromptForEntry`, and bvb `addEntry` calls therefore fire when the key is pressed. Nothing depends on them firing earlier. Closing the tab during the freeze skips score recording — acceptable. - The freeze is purely additive: all winner resolution, high-score saving, and leaderboard logic inside `endGame`/`endGameTwoPlayer` runs unchanged. - Iron Snake interior void deaths, overhead view, and both-snakes-dying-on-the-same-tick (draw / head-to-head) are all handled by the render/bounds logic. - The death marker sorts correctly relative to the head and tail graphics (head drawn at 1.6× body height, tail drawn as a tapered wedge) introduced in the same commit, because all three are entries in the same painter's-algorithm list.
